name: AGU A Ramen Bistro
tagline: Solid Honolulu ramen chain with rich tonkotsu broth — reliable bowl every single time.
mustOrder: Kotteri ramen — thick, rich tonkotsu broth with chashu and soft egg
AGU shoyu ramen — soy-based broth, lighter but still flavorful
Gyoza — pan-fried crispy, good starter while you wait
parking: Strip mall parking lot. The McCully location usually has spots available.
talkStory: Founded by Chef Hideaki Ariga, who spent years perfecting his tonkotsu recipe in Japan before launching AGU in Hawaii around 2013. The name means 'jaw' in Japanese, a nod to the collagen-rich pork jowl bones that give the broth its silky richness. Now with multiple Oahu locations, it's the island's most reliable ramen chain. Order the kotteri (extra-thick) broth and customize your richness level — late-night hours make it a solid post-bar bowl.
